apify_person_profile_items.description:
I’m an author, keynote speaker, executive coach, leadership strategist, and Top 100 HR Influencer who helps organizations treat leadership development as a business investment, not a nice-to-have initiative.

As founder of WorkJoy, I partner with C-suite executives, founders, and leadership teams navigating growth, transformation, and complexity. Together we build leadership capability that drives measurable performance, strengthens culture, and supports long-term strategy. My clients are ambitious and capable. They are not looking for inspiration alone. They want traction. We turn strategic priorities into aligned leadership behavior and sustained results.

Drawing on more than two decades of experience across corporate sales, operations, and HR, I bring a practical, commercially grounded lens to leadership development. I help leaders see patterns, cut through noise, and focus on the decisions and behaviors that move the business forward. Clients describe my style as direct, insightful, and deeply human. Outcomes include sharper focus, stronger executive presence, clearer communication, and teams that perform with accountability and resilience.

I design and deliver executive coaching, leadership development strategy, manager capability programs, and talent pipeline programs that shift behavior and strengthen organizational effectiveness. I advise companies on succession planning, culture transformation, and performance alignment, ensuring the right leaders are equipped to do the right work in the right ways.

As a keynote speaker and facilitator, I challenge audiences at global professional associations and national corporate events to rethink how leadership is developed and funded in an era defined by rapid technological change and rising human complexity. Learn more at https://www.tinarobinsonspeaks.com.

My book, Developing Your Business Leaders: A Guide to Investing at All Levels (ATD Press, March 17, 2026), helps workplace investors, from founders to front-line managers, build the business case for leadership investment so the right people do the right things in the right ways to achieve optimal results. Pre-order here: https://a.co/d/00wuaLyX

My clients span technology, energy, financial services, higher education, retail, nonprofit, consulting, and consumer products. Different industries. One shared truth: leadership capability determines performance.

If leadership is your competitive advantage, let’s talk.

education.university_name: University of Michigan - Stephen M. Ross School of Business

job_description:
WorkJoy (https://www.workjoycoaching.com/) is a global leadership development firm specializing in executive and leadership coaching, leadership training, offsite facilitation, keynote speaking, and fractional talent development support. 

We partner with executives, founders, HR leaders, and talent development teams to strengthen leadership capability, elevate performance, and build cultures where people and business results thrive.

Our work spans the full talent lifecycle, from first-time managers to senior executives, helping leaders develop the skills, behaviors, and confidence required to navigate complexity, change, and growth. Clients engage WorkJoy for practical, business-aligned leadership solutions that translate directly into better decisions, stronger teams, and measurable impact.

WorkJoy supports organizations across healthcare, technology, nonprofit, consumer brands, professional services, and mission-driven sectors. Our clients represent multiple industries and include leaders and teams from BEIS, CNM LLP, FOX Sports, Frederick Fisher and Partners, Ingenovis, KEYS Nonprofit, LRW, National Wildlife Federation, PATTERN Beauty, Plug In Ventures, Pressed Juicery, ReCharge, SAP, Spin Master, Spotify, Thermo Fisher Scientific, and The Whole Child.

Our insights on leadership, culture, and talent development are regularly featured at industry-leading conferences and events, including:

• SHRM Annual Conferences (2022–2026)
• ATD International Conference & Exposition (2020–2026)
• BambooHR Virtual Summit (2018–2024)
• DisruptHR (2019–2024)
• LinkedIn Talent Connect (2019)
• Association for Animal Welfare Advancement Annual Conference (2023)
• Veterinary Hospital Managers Association Annual Conference (2025)

If your organization is serious about leadership development, employee engagement, and turning talent investment into real business value, WorkJoy meets you where strategy and humanity intersect.

Visit us at https://www.workjoycoaching.com/
experience.job_location: Greater Los Angeles, California, United States

job_description:
Pub date March 17th, 2026. Available for pre-order today! 

Per Amazon: Invest in Leaders at All Levels as a Key Part of Your Business Strategy

Drawing on nearly three decades of experience across sales operations, technology consulting, and talent development, Tina Schust Robinson offers a much-needed reimagining of leadership development. She guides all leadership investors—from founders, executives, and frontline managers to those doing the hands-on design and delivery work—toward a more holistic and successful development approach.

Making a powerful business case for investing intentionally in leaders, Robinson explores why leadership investment is a crucial aspect of any business strategy and what characteristics define a good leader. She shows how leadership investors can craft initiatives that are tied to their organization’s and employees’ specific needs, sustainable over time, and highly effective. Moving beyond quick fixes and one-off programs, Robinson makes a practical case for why investing intentionally in leaders is a crucial aspect of any business strategy.

This book shares Robinson’s one-to-one, one-to-many, and one-to-all leadership investment framework. It demonstrates how organizations can pursue coaching and mentoring for individuals, improve leadership training for groups, and develop a comprehensive strategy to address leadership development throughout the talent life cycle.

Interviews with successful corporate and nonprofit leaders and talent development pros bring the three-pronged framework into action. Checklists, assessments, and exercises allow leadership investors to apply the framework immediately and tailor their solutions to specific audiences and business needs.

Whether you’re leading a team or shaping an entire organization, this book will change how you think about leadership—and the return it can deliver when you invest wisely.

job_description:
Recruited to create global leadership development and in-house coaching function at pioneering incubator of digitally native fashion brands, including Yitty, JustFab, ShoeDazzle, FabKids, and Savage X Fenty. 

Achievements include leadership content creation + facilitation, offsite design + facilitation, executive coaching, management development programming, professional upskilling and learning initiatives, career development, succession planning, talent reviews, high-potential leader identification and program creation, mentor program development and launch, and assessments (360s, CliftonStrengths, DiSC, conflict style, etc.). 

Partnered with leaders and employees in the U.S., United Kingdom, Germany, and Spain.

featured.subtitle: Fast Company
featured.text: Interviewed (again!) for a Fast Company article, this one on finding and staying in the zone. "...My recommendation for staying “in the zone” is to identify what this zone feels like and to recognize when you enter and leave it. Make your zone real for you—and ignore everyone else’s advice for maintaining high performance throughout the day....Manage your time as the gift that it is."
featured.title: From Fast Company - Expert advice on maintaining your mental edge

featured.subtitle: Fast Company
featured.text: "AI isn’t the destination. It’s a method of reaching it. It is the way we clear mental clutter and speed up the work that bogs us down. When we define AI as a resource and a tool rather than an identity, the whole conversation shifts, allowing us to move faster, solve problems with greater precision, and spend more time on work that advances us personally and professionally."
featured.title: Why AI skills are the new gold standard for job seekers

featured.subtitle: ATD
featured.text: A practical how-to for creating a business case for coaching that yields ROI. Thank you to ATD for publishing!
featured.title: Making the Business Case for Coaching

featured.subtitle: Fast Company
featured.text: "...Bottom line: you’re not starting over. You’re building forward. Ground yourself in where you’ve been, learn what’s ahead, and construct bridges between the two. That’s how you climb your next mountain, starting from base camp and not from scratch."
featured.title: Fast Company article: "How to transition to a new industry without starting over"

featured.subtitle: ATD
featured.text: Wrote blog for ATD about how to re-imagine giving and receiving feedback.
featured.title: Three Ways Leaders Can Reframe Feedback

apify_person_profile_items.profile_headline: Leadership Strategist ✷ Author of Developing Your Business Leaders: A Guide to Investing at All Levels ✷ Keynote Speaker ✷ Executive Coach ✷ Top 100 HR Influencer ✷ Fractional Talent Development Executive

publication.summary:
Tina Schust Robinson offers a much-needed reimagining of leadership development. She guides all talent investors - from founders and executives committed to the growth of their people to those doing the hands-on design, building, and delivery work - toward a more holistic and successful leadership development approach. Making a powerful business case for investing intentionally in leaders, Robinson explores why leadership investment is a crucial aspect of any business strategy and what characteristics define a good leader. She shows how talent development professionals and teams can craft initiatives that are tied to their organization’s and employees’ specific needs, sustainable over time, and highly effective.

Robinson also shares her one-to-one, one-to-many, and one-to-all leadership development framework. She demonstrates how organizations can pursue coaching and mentoring for individuals, improve leadership training for groups, and develop a comprehensive organizational strategy to address leadership development throughout the talent life cycle (from recruiting and onboarding to performance and goal management) through development, succession planning, and rewards and retention.

Practical and accessible, Robinson’s framework includes tools and exercises to help practitioners tailor their solutions to specific audiences and business needs. In each chapter, she shares insights from interviews with successful leaders and talent development pros to show the three-pronged framework in action. Checklists, assessments, and questionnaires allow you to apply the framework immediately.
publication.title: BOOK = Developing Your Business Leaders: A Guide to Investing at All Levels

publication.source: Association for Talent Development - TD Magazine

recommendations_given.description: As a consultant, we dream of a client that has a strong vision, a clearly-defined definition of success, and willingness to listen and co-create to achieve shared goals. With Abigail this dream become reality. We were introduced by a mutual colleague, and Abigail engaged me to help her bring to life a modularized management development program for LRW. Building on successes and misses from other programs, Abigail outlined her ideas for the curriculum, encouraging my input and giving me the space to put to paper our ideas. I so enjoyed our content reviews, as they always resulted in a stronger product. Abigail knows her stakeholders, the business strategy, and the LRW culture, ensuring our program would be well-received and value-added. She launched the program this year to high praise from managers. I can't wait for the next opportunity to partner with Abigail!
recommendations_given.name: Abigail Boudreaux
recommendations_given.subtitle: Professional Certified Executive Coach | I help individuals and teams navigate change and work in more fully human ways

recommendations_given.context: Tina Schust worked with Tom but on different teams
recommendations_given.description: If you seek a strategic yet hands-on executive to drive successful business outcomes and improve how the company operates, you've found him. Tom has a knack for transforming complexity into rational simplicity and then plans into action. Tom and I both were peer success coaches at SAP and worked together to deploy the global operating model improving sales and marketing alignment. He's a trusted colleague with a unique ability to work across organizational silos, bringing knowledge, insights, enthusiasm, and humor.
recommendations_given.name: Tom Sorge, MBA, CDMP
recommendations_given.subtitle: AI-driven leader blending experience with intelligent tools to streamline work, enhance revenue processes, and train AI agents that amplify human impact.

recommendations_given.context: Tina Schust worked with Natalie but they were at different companies
recommendations_given.description: I've had the pleasure of working with Natalie as a speaker and panel moderator for General Assembly. She is an accomplished event planner, able to find the right people with harmonious backgrounds to come together to tell a cohesive, engaging story. She empowers her moderator and speakers, puttings us at ease and bringing out our best stories. With all the competing events in Los Angeles and Santa Monica, I applaud Natalie's positivity and her determination to make General Assembly the go-to place for learning about the workplace of the future.
recommendations_given.name: Natalie Dao
recommendations_given.subtitle: HRBP Marketing & Creative at Liquid Death

recommendations_given.context: Tina Schust was senior to Niveditha but didn't manage Niveditha directly
recommendations_given.description: I had the pleasure of working closely with Niveditha to build the analytics team for SuccessFactors' Business Transformation Advisory function. Although new to the workforce technology space when hired, Niveditha brought a passion for learning, education in HR, and  detailed technical understanding of other SAP products. I was impressed by how quickly Niveditha became a go-to expert in business case analysis, especially the more complicated stories (global companies, different divisions opting for different solutions). She earned the trust of a seasoned team through her commitment to excellence, openness to feedback, and creativity grounded in technical knowledge and practical story telling. It was a joy to be Niveditha's colleague at SuccessFactors, and know she will continue to add tremendous value to any project team.
recommendations_given.name: Niveditha Kesavan
recommendations_given.subtitle: Senior Director, SAP Business Network GTM Strategy & Operations | Advisory | Innovation | Technology

recommendations_given.context: Tina Schust worked with Betsy but they were at different companies
recommendations_given.description: I had the pleasure of watching Betsy in action as a guest lecturer in my organizational development class at California State University, Long Beach. Betsy had 35 students mesmerized as she described her career path from JPL to Disney to TOMS, and then engaged the class in a vibrant discussion about employee development challenges at TOMS. She asked specific questions to pull creative insights from the students, and openly brainstormed about how their recommendations could directly contribute to business success at TOMS. I so appreciate Betsy's enthusiasm for her work and for the larger field of talent development - she inspired me and the class and I look forward to having her return to the classroom.
recommendations_given.name: Betsy Lopez-Riley
recommendations_given.subtitle: Driving Organizational Transformation | Nordstrom, TOMS, Disney, NASA

recommendations_given.context: Tina Schust worked with Michelle on the same team
recommendations_given.description: I had the pleasure of working with Michelle as she led the Australia-Pacific-Japan regional team of value engineers for SAP's human capital management (SuccessFactors) line of business. I admired and was inspired by her creativity, her forthright communication style, and how she empowered her team. She hired smart, agile people and provided the right support to help them be successful and to let them shine. While leading a large team within a large region, Michelle also was an energetic collaborator on global best practices. While I miss having her as a colleague at SAP I am excited for her new organizations, as they will benefit from her intelligence, insight, and grace.
recommendations_given.name: Michelle Botha
recommendations_given.subtitle: Founder, The Gifted Crown Company

recommendations_given.context: Tina Schust reported directly to Ken
recommendations_given.description: I've had the pleasure of working with and for Ken Pearson during our time at SuccessFactors. As a colleague, Ken was collaborative and generous with his time, quick to share his deep knowledge of talent management honed at Walmart. As the VP of our team, Ken led by example, inspiring us to live to our potential, balance our professional and personal lives, and cross regional and functional boundaries to do the right thing. Ken has a passion for people development, and I am one of many people who have benefited from Ken's mentoring. He has the rare ability to hold one to a high expectation and yet make you feel completely supported and valued as you reach for those heights. I am grateful to know Ken and look forward to our continued partnership.
recommendations_given.name: Ken Pearson, GPHR, ACC, CPC  (He / Him)
recommendations_given.subtitle: Executive Coach & Culture Architect | HR Transformation, Talent Strategy & Leadership Development Advisor | Organizational Culture, Change Management & Inclusive Workplace Expert

recommendations_given.context: Tina Schust worked with Marites on the same team
recommendations_given.description: I worked with Marites on the MedAssets HR team for almost five years. During that time I watched her evolve from a coordinator to a generalist to a benefits management expert, supporting larger and larger employee populations as MedAssets grew through acquisition. Marites can handle whatever is thrown her way, from mastering remote meeting technology so she could deliver orientation across the enterprise to absorbing different state employment laws to accommodate the latest acquisition. She digs into the challenge and seeks the necessary training and knowledge to achieve success. Marites has the unique ability to truly balance the HR roles of "employee advocate" and "business partner" - employees and managers alike trust her judgment and follow her guidance, even if they would like to see Marites lean a bit more on their side. With her dedication to human resources, her detail orientation and thoroughness, unflagging ethical and moral code, and warm sense of humor, Marites is an exemplary team member and an asset to any organization.
recommendations_given.name: Marites Foster
recommendations_given.subtitle: Client Services Manager

recommendations_given.context: Tina Schust worked with Lisa but they were at different companies
recommendations_given.description: Lisa has served as my professional coach and mentor, showing wisdom and sensitivity in every interaction. Not only does she actively listen during our coaching sessions, but Lisa also holds me accountable for making the changes we discuss - pushing me when I need the nudge and congratulating for a job well done to encourage  Her practical, results-oriented approach to coaching has yielded both professional and personal successes - Lisa has helped me recognize challenges and turn "problems" into opportunities for discovery and transformation. I always will be grateful to Lisa for the time and energy she has invested in me and my journey.
recommendations_given.name: Lisa Sheehan
recommendations_given.subtitle: Strategic Global Human Resources Leader I Expertise in Positive Employee Relations I Transformational Servant Leader & Business Partner

recommendations_given.context: Tina Schust worked with Leslie on the same team
recommendations_given.description:
I had the pleasure of working with Leslie Lynn at the Ross School of Business Office of Career Development. Not only does she excel at the one-on-one coaching (of students, alumni, and colleagues alike), she shines as a presenter in front of large groups. Leslie makes it look easy - she has the gift of gently nudging someone down a path of insight so that someone thinks the career "ah hah" moments were all her own. Countless Ross graduates have her to thank for clarified career paths and life journeys. 

Leslie cares deeply about her staff, encouraging their creativity and professional growth, Her team members have gone onto varied careers within and outside the University, crediting Leslie's leadership and inspiration as critical to their success.

I miss working with Leslie, and consider myself lucky to count her as a colleague and a friend.
recommendations_given.name: Leslie Lynn
recommendations_given.subtitle: Director, MBA Career Coaching at University of Michigan Ross School CDO

recommendations_given.context: Tina Schust worked with Brian on the same team
recommendations_given.description: Brian is an excellent recruiter. His easy-going style puts candidates at ease, and his passion for his company results in most candidates desperately wanting to receive (and accept) an offer. It was a joy working with Brian, and I still call him for recruiting advice. Brian's network has no equal, and I am honored to be part of it. Overall...I just adore Mr. Ellenberger and am thrilled to endorse him.
recommendations_given.name: Brian Ellenberger, MA, VSP
recommendations_given.subtitle: Talent Acquisition Professional with Full-Cycle Recruiting Experience, Strategic Sourcer in Technology and Healthcare Organizations

recommendations_given.description: My company has used Ed as a source of hard-to find healthcare management talent for the past several years. The candidates he recommends are solid and experienced. Ed's extensive network is a valuable resource for us, and we appreciate his efforts to match our specific business needs with the right candidates. Thanks Ed!
recommendations_given.name: Ed Simmons
recommendations_given.subtitle: Senior Recruiter/CEO at Belle Oaks of America

recommendations_received.context: Daniel was Tina Schust's client
recommendations_received.description: Tina has been instrumental in guiding me through complex workplace and career challenges. Her coaching process offers a strong and efficient sounding board for making difficult decisions in a short amount of time. Beyond being an outstanding coach, she brings a deep understanding of human nature and how people work, making her guidance both practical and empathetic.
recommendations_received.name: Daniel Mendelzon
recommendations_received.subtitle: Digital Transformation | Visiting Fellow at MIT Sloan School of Management | MIT MBA | Ex-JPMorgan | Credit Products | Risk Management

recommendations_received.context: Lacy was Tina Schust's client
recommendations_received.description: Tina is a wonderfully engaging facilitator! She provided significant value at our Annual Leadership Conference presenting on Imposter Syndrome and Productive Conflict. She was generous with her time and worked 1-on-1 with one of our teams. My HR team attended her SHRM 24 sessions and knew we needed to share her expertise with our staff. Highly recommend. 5 stars!
recommendations_received.name: Lacy Diaz
recommendations_received.subtitle: Director of Organizational Development and Services at Golden Spread Electric Cooperative, Inc.

recommendations_received.context: Christiane was Tina Schust's client
recommendations_received.description: We hired Tina to facilitate two workshops using the DiSC personality profile assessment, one with our leadership team and the other for all employees. Tina was a thoughtful partner, aligning her expertise with the unique needs of our organization to deliver content that felt personalized to our culture and mission. She absorbed feedback and tailored materials to best honor our founder’s priorities. As a result, our team walked away with deeper insights about their own go-to behaviors and how to adapt to meet others through more effective relationships. The workshops were high energy, with just the right balance of instruction, discussion, and humor. I highly recommend Tina as a facilitator and consultant.
recommendations_received.name: Christiane Pendarvis
recommendations_received.subtitle: Chief Executive | Brand President | Board Member | Speaker | Omni-Channel and DTC Retail Expert

recommendations_received.context: Luis worked with Tina Schust but on different teams
recommendations_received.description: Tina helped me elevate my executive presence as I prepared for a promotion to senior VP. She coordinated a robust 360 at various levels of the organization. She coached me the opportunities it revealed while reminding me of my strengths. She was tough on me and I trusted her to be honest. Tina’s coaching revealed my blind spots, allowing me to address them and shift behaviors with my team members, peers, and especially other executives. The benefits of the coaching extended to my team, as I expanded my capacity to invest in them by practicing many things Tina taught me. I don’t know that I could have gained the trust of the executive team in the timeframe I did without her guidance. I am grateful to Tina for her support, humor, and wisdom during my growth and recommend her to any leader in transition.
recommendations_received.name: Luis Velazquez

recommendations_received.context: Sam worked with Tina Schust on the same team
recommendations_received.description:
I had the pleasure of hosting Tina Robinson as a speaker on the People Strategy Forum podcast, where we had a thought-provoking discussion on owning our power and unleashing our true potential.

Tina's extensive experience at the intersection of humans, technology, and work truly shines through. Her corporate background, directing global change management initiatives and transforming team cultures, gives her a unique perspective on aligning people, processes, and technology with business priorities.

During our conversation, Tina eloquently explored the concept of owning our power and provided a four-step process to help us visualize, identify, initiate, and integrate our power. Her insights on visualization and the power of choice were particularly enlightening.

Beyond her expertise, Tina's passion for being a keynote speaker is evident. Her ability to engage and inspire an audience is truly remarkable. Her dedication to ongoing personal and professional growth is also noteworthy, as she is an honors graduate and actively involved in international nonprofits.

I highly recommend Tina Robinson as a speaker, coach, and thought leader. Her ability to guide individuals and organizations in unlocking their true power is invaluable. Working with Tina will undoubtedly lead to personal and professional growth, as she empowers individuals to embrace their strengths and achieve greater success.
recommendations_received.name: Sam Reeve
recommendations_received.subtitle: Global Compensation & Total Rewards Strategist | Helping CEOs & CHROs Build Fair, Competitive, People-Centric Pay Systems | Founder, CompTeam

recommendations_received.context: Aisling was Tina Schust's client
recommendations_received.description: We engaged Tina to deliver goal-setting workshops across North America as part of a larger corporate performance management initiative. As a result of these engaging events, our team members felt more comfortable having goal setting discussions with their leaders, better understood their accountability in career development, and more actively submitted goals and career aspirations through Workday. We already have launched targeted learning and development programs in response to these entries. Given the intensity of work schedules, our team members are discerning about how they spend their time and demand training that is engaging, value-added, and "real." They described the goal-setting workshops as "dynamic, practical and fun" - high praise! Tina is easy to partner with and truly cares about the success of our programs, and I am grateful for her creativity, passion, and deep knowledge of the learning space. I look forward to partnering with Tina on more projects soon, not just in North America but across our global locations.

recommendations_received.subtitle: | People & Talent Leader | Consultant | Educator | Career Coach I Enabling People and Organizations Grow and Develop | Talks about #talent, #careers, #leadership, #employeeengagement, and #employeexperience

recommendations_received.context: Abigail was Tina Schust's client
recommendations_received.description:
I engaged Tina to co-create a customized development program for our people managers and am thrilled with the results! She was quick to understand our LRW culture and the kind of content and approach that would work best for our employees. After an initial brainstorming session where we defined objectives, Tina leveraged her depth of learning design and facilitation experience to create a big-picture framework, then draft topical session content, ultimately forming a cohesive curriculum for us to bring to life in the classroom. 

She was a true partner during the process, hearing our goals, offering fresh ideas and quickly building on feedback my L&D team and I shared. Tina has a pragmatic approach to learning, which shined through the program presentations and activities. I especially enjoyed our "sounding board" sessions where we reviewed decks together, engaged in inspiring L&D discussions and co-edited to hone our final product. 

Tina was referred to me by a friend, and I’m glad we had the opportunity to work together! I look forward to continued partnership with Tina in the future, leveraging her skills as a learning strategist, talent consultant, and executive coach.

recommendations_received.subtitle: Professional Certified Executive Coach | I help individuals and teams navigate change and work in more fully human ways

recommendations_received.context: Carla M was Tina Schust's client
recommendations_received.description: We engaged Tina in 2017 to help the FamileSCN2A Foundation create its first strategic plan. Through her patient yet focused and deliberate exploration of vision, mission, and values, our board members began to unite as a team, more willing to listen fully and to tackle the tough questions about who we are and what we imagine is possible. Tina leveraged her deep expertise in navigating organizations to walk us through a thorough skills gap assessment, board roles and responsibilities, and even interview guides to support our conversations with prospective team members. Thanks to her we added two amazing members who elevated our board to new levels. Most recently, in late 2018, we asked Tina to facilitate a critical board meeting. Once again, she patiently navigated us through a calibration and validation of our mission, the creation of a SWOT analysis, and a deep dive into the needs - and action plans - of our committees. In between group events Tina gently coached individual board members, applauding our progress, reminding us of our strengths and unique abilities, and encouraging us to own our power. Our foundation is stronger for having Tina as our coach, consultant, facilitator, and friend - and we recommend her to any organization and its leader ready to rise to and unleash full potential.
recommendations_received.name: Carla M Forbes
recommendations_received.subtitle: Non-Profit Administrative Manager | FamilieSCN2A Foundation Co-Founder | Rare Disease Advocacy Expert | Co-Chair of the Pediatric Patient and Family Advisory Council – Baystate Children’s Hospital

recommendations_received.context: Judithe was Tina Schust's client
recommendations_received.description:
Tina facilitated a multi-month virtual manager training program for our Financial Services (Kaiser NCAL) mid-level managers.  
She kicked off the program with a vibrant in-person workshop that not only engaged the diverse audience but primed them for the learning they were to receive over the next few months. Every two weeks she lead an online session that balanced content with team application and personal reflection.  
Tina quickly reads her audience and adjusts the flow to meet shifting learning needs. She knows the content so well that she can go "off script," injecting humor and weaving in activities that make the discussion "real."   I welcome the opportunity to have Tina train and coach additional Financial Services (Kaiser) team members and highly recommend her to organizations ready to jump start their L&D programs.

recommendations_received.subtitle: Senior Learning Consultant at TPMG Financial Services - Kaiser Permanente (Retired)

recommendations_received.description:
Tina has been facilitating a multi-month leadership program for our mid-level (mostly technology and product) managers. Following her theme of "building your leadership toolbox," Tina effortlessly weaves together engaging content, straightforward models, hands-on application, and personal reflection to ensure value-driven "ah hahs." She offers herself as a coach to her students, usually staying late to for one-on-one talks to make the training "real" and making herself available outside of the "classroom" 

From an HR perspective I have seen great improvement in our managers' ability to effectively communicate with diverse stakeholders, navigate conflict, deliver feedback - and huge leaps in the managers' self-awareness and feeling confident in their roles which was the reason for the providing the training initially. 

Tina has also provided me insightful observations from her time here at iHerb.  I enthusiastically recommend Tina as a trainer, coach, and workplace partner - she brings wide-ranging experience, a strategic mindset to humans in the workplace, engaging wit, and a unique ability to connect with teams and individuals with her one of a kind personality! 

Excited for future training sessions with Tina

recommendations_received.subtitle: CHRO/Chief People Officer with Start-up and Turnaround Experience in Healthcare and Healthcare Technology. Helping Organizations attract and retain Talent.

recommendations_received.description: Tina is extraordinary when it comes to her dedication and ability as a human resources professional She is a great role model for the company values - compassion, character, confident and commitment. I've had the pleasure to work with Tina for almost five years. Her charisma and passion for everything that she does speaks for itself.
recommendations_received.name: Marites Foster
recommendations_received.subtitle: Client Services Manager

recommendations_received.context: Vicki was senior to Tina Schust but didn't manage Tina Schust directly
recommendations_received.description: Tina is a very talented organizational development and HR professional.  As the HR Professional assigned to support our team, Tina worked very closely with our managers to develop career plans, conduct training classes, coach managers, and help manage communication to staff.  She can move successfully from trainer to HR advisor to department manager.  Tina is professional in all her interactions with others.  She is committed to exceeding expectations of her customers.  And, she is a pleasure to work with on a daily basis. Tina would be an asset to any HR or Organizational Development team.
recommendations_received.name: Vicki Miller
recommendations_received.subtitle: Principal, Palm Key Associates, LLC

recommendations_received.context: Christine reported directly to Tina Schust
recommendations_received.description: I worked with Tina as both my manager and colleague supporting two business units. In both capacities she has always gone above and beyond and set a positive example for those she works with. She has a passion for following through in projects and bringing everyone together. She's been able to implement solutions and inspire creatively by solving issues outside of the traditional path when needed. Tina's enthusiastic, dedicated and genuinely a nice person to work with.
recommendations_received.name: Christine Shaw
recommendations_received.subtitle: Strategic client solutions leader with consultative sales focus. Passionate about making a meaningful impact within the healthcare ecosystem.

recommendations_received.context: Leslie worked with Tina Schust on the same team
recommendations_received.description: Tina is a great manager with a drive to really push the organization's performance and systems to the best level. She is a great strategic thinker, but she didn't hesitate to jump in to help solve a problem, meet a deadline, or handle a delicate issue with a student or staff member. She is very diligent in her work and very versatile in her contributions, depending on the needs at the time whether it be helping a recruiter, or giving feedback to a student. She works to develop herself while continues to grow other staff members with her mentorship.  I would love to have her on my team again!
recommendations_received.name: Leslie Lynn
recommendations_received.subtitle: Director, MBA Career Coaching at University of Michigan Ross School CDO

recommendations_received.context: Brian worked with Tina Schust on the same team
recommendations_received.description: TIna is a load of fun to work with and really engages great HR initiatives with her internal stakeholders.  She is probably the best trainer or presenter I have ever seen.  She engages her audience at all levels.  I miss working with her.

update_data.content: Today we’re announcing our quarterly magazine, “RV Recap”, featuring highlights from our work withBRCĒ,Obatala Sciences, andLutheran Social Services of the National Capital Area! We also look back atThe Idea Village's 3rd Coast Venture Summit andPitchBreakfast, whereCian RobinsonandKelsey Gibbonscontinued to bring our mission to life.

This city once changed how the world moves.

Now, it’s happening again, this time in the sky.

From the 11th floor ofMichigan Central, this photo showsblueflite’s COBALT flying high over Detroit.

It’s part of our continued push to deploy advanced drone technology across healthcare, automotive and dual use applications.

update_data.content:
As more people turn to supplements to support long-term health, researchers are working to better understand what those choices really mean for the body and brain.

Scientists at the MUSC, including Dr. Onder Albayram and Dr. Eda Karakaya, are leading that effort with new findings on fish oil supplementation and brain recovery after repetitive mild traumatic brain injury.

Published in Cell Reports, the study highlights why biological context matters and opens the door to more informed and personalized approaches to brain health.

Read the full story:https://lnkd.in/eJ7VGF59#ChangingWhatsPossible

update_data.content:
🏆 Obatala Sciences Named to American Business Vanguard-2026! 🏆

We are honored to announce thatObatala Scienceshas been recognized as the 2026 Vanguard Biotechnology Business of the Year, byGPMG. This achievement reflects the extraordinary work of our Obatala team, whose dedication and scientific excellence drive everything we do. It also represents the confidence and collaboration of our investors, partners, supporters, and community members who share our belief in what’s possible when innovation is guided by purpose.

At Obatala Sciences, our mission is clear: to harness preclinical insights to help bring safe, efficacious therapies to patients—making them accessible to all who need them, regardless of their backgrounds. This recognition reinforces our commitment to advancing translational science in ways that improve lives and strengthen the future of healthcare.

We are deeply grateful to the person who nominated us and to everyone who has supported our journey. This honor belongs to our entire community, and it inspires us to keep building, discovering, and delivering impact together.

Thank you for being part of the Obatala mission.

Check out the list of Vanguard winners here:#ObatalaSciences#Biotechnology#LifeSciences#BiotechInnovation#PreclinicalResearch#HealthEquity#VanguardAwards#TeamScience

AtLutheran Social Services of the National Capital Area, we believe in a whole person approach to well-being, and that's why we are launching Dignity Behavioral Health. We are grateful to partner withFundamental Administrative Solutionsin this endeavor so we can focus on what we do best. 

Our licensed, bilingual clinicians are trained in trauma-informed treatment modalities, are available evening hours, and offer individual, family, and couples counseling. We are able to serve all ages.
